message.content:
=== THE LAW ===
A source enters the corpus ONLY bound to real bytes via provenance.py (manifestation_id + content_hash).
Under a JOURNAL-ONLY contract it must be journal-attributable. A working paper / preprint / ACCEPTED
MANUSCRIPT is NOT the journal version -- peer review changes numbers (Acemoglu's robot effect: 0.37pp in
the NBER WP, 0.2pp in the published JPE). `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` must stay GREEN;
NEVER weaken a check to make it pass. WE DO NOT USE SCI-HUB/LIBGEN -- only legitimate green-OA.

=== TONIGHT'S LESSON, EARNED FOUR TIMES ===
THE BUILDER CANNOT VERIFY ITSELF. Modules self-tested green while fabrication shipped; provenance.py
passed 18/18 while the P0 ran live on disk; the canary went 16/16 while 6 attacks succeeded. So: the
agent that builds does NOT verify. A separate adversary attacks.


=== SOL'S DESIGN IS AT /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_FULLTEXT_LANE_V9.md — READ IT FIRST. IT IS THE BUILD CONTRACT. DO NOT REDESIGN IT. ===

Why this lane: our corpus is TEN journal-attributable works. cellcog (#1) has ~98 sources. The argument
planner (the biggest score lever) found ZERO genuine cross-source conflicts in 10 papers -- it is STARVED.
Sol's yield forecast for the ~2,490 no-known-OA candidates: 450-850 complete documents, 180-360 STRICT
journal-VoR-attributable, best estimate ~260 additional strict journal full texts. The three biggest lanes
(CORE 60-140, OpenAIRE 45-110, OAI-PMH 35-90) ARE ALL UNBUILT.

Discovery is DONE: ~5,061 candidates in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/ (frontier_2023_2025_FINAL,
oa_cell_discovery, canon_and_contrasts_leads, citation-graph). ~2,571 already carry an offline OA URL.
Existing tools: acquisition.py (the ONE network door), provenance.py, event_ledger.py, source_router.py +
config/source_routes.yaml, deep_fetch.py, weighting.py.


YOUR TASK — SOL'S SECTION "Decision" + §4 + the route-lineage bug. THIS IS A LIVE FABRICATION PATH. Close
it BEFORE any volume is added.

 1. `accepted_manuscript_of` is currently in SPAN_PRESERVING (provenance.py:216). REMOVE IT.
    version_align.py:220 maps 'acceptedVersion' to a span-preserving relationship — STOP THAT.
    alignment_census.py:140 declares accepted manuscripts journal-admissible from repository METADATA —
    STOP THAT. "An accepted manuscript is NEVER the journal version merely because a repository says
    acceptedVersion."

 2. PER-SPAN ATTRIBUTION (Sol §4). Change the API from manifestation-wide permission to binding-specific:
        resolve_attribution(binding_id, attribution_policy)
    A SpanCorrespondence must carry: source manifestation_id + raw/text hash + offsets + verbatim span;
    target manifestation_id + raw/text hash + offsets + verbatim span; canonicalization algorithm AND
    version; exact canonical-span hash; identity decisions for both manifestations.
    The verifier RECHECKS both hashes, both offsets, and exact canonical text equality.
    Consequences Sol requires: repository metadata alone can NEVER prove span equivalence; a span found
    independently in VoR bytes should be REBOUND directly to the VoR manifestation; a correspondence
    grants permission for THAT SPAN ONLY; "a disagreement such as 0.37 versus 0.2 fails immediately";
    expression-wide exact_copy_of is reserved for identity-confirmed manifestations whose ENTIRE canonical
    text is equal; an accepted manuscript with no VoR bytes stays accepted-manuscript-attributable only.

 3. ROUTE-LINEAGE BUG: source_router.py can credit an adapter with a manifestation fetched through a
    DIFFERENT adapter, because it reduces all work-level manifestations instead of its own candidate
    lineage. candidate_id must follow the whole chain: resolver request -> candidate -> content request ->
    redirects -> manifestation. Add the ResolveContext / DocumentCandidate / Manifestation records from
    Sol §1. "The adapter must not write FULLTEXT, THIS_WORK, VERSION_OF_RECORD, ADMISSIBLE, or an
    expression edge" — those are REDUCER outputs.

 4. Remove the flat-corpus write in deep_fetch.py:185 (it admits anything except CITATION_ONLY, truncates
    text at 120k chars, and discards the manifestation identity the law needs).

 5. WRONG-WORK + COMPLETENESS (Sol §5): identity from the FETCHED CONTENT (JATS article-DOI/title/
    contributors; PDF front-matter DOI/title/byline with repository COVER SHEETS SEGMENTED from article
    front matter; HTML citation meta + visible header). CONFIRMED / DIFFERENT_WORK / UNRESOLVED per his
    decision rules — and note his correction: "a generic title without an author must NOT produce
    DIFFERENT_WORK; the current event reducer is too aggressive." Completeness is artifact-specific: a
    short legal judgment is COMPLETE; a registry record needs no prose.

Run `python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` after every change (must stay green). Report file:line for
every change and what you RAN to verify. Do NOT commit.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	No code was changed. This is the build contract for Opus.
2	
3	## Decision
4	
5	Build a resolver-to-manifestation lane with four hard boundaries:
6	
7	1. Repository adapters only discover `DocumentCandidate` records.
8	2. A generic acquisition executor fetches and hashes the bytes.
9	3. Reducers derive identity, completeness, version, and admissibility from those bytes.
10	4. Attribution permission is granted per verified span, never from an accepted-manuscript label.
11	
12	Before adding volume, close the current P0:
13	
14	- Remove `accepted_manuscript_of` from `SPAN_PRESERVING` in [provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py:216).
15	- Stop mapping `acceptedVersion` to a span-preserving relationship in [version_align.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/version_align.py:220).
16	- Stop declaring accepted manuscripts journal-admissible merely from repository metadata in [alignment_census.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/alignment_census.py:140).
17	
18	An accepted manuscript is admissible only when the contract permits accepted manuscripts, and attribution must name that manifestation. It is never the journal version merely because a repository says `acceptedVersion`.
19	
20	Two verification corrections:
21	
22	- `source_routes.yaml` is syntactically valid: 25 route IDs, no duplicate IDs. My interim duplicate-arXiv warning was caused by overlapping truncated output. The problem is missing executable adapter wiring, not malformed YAML.
23	- The named `outputs/acquisition_campaign/` contains discovery artifacts, not the 5,061-unit full-text dead-run ledger. I therefore accept the operator’s 2,490-candidate census as an input, but the yield figures below are forecasts, not measurements reconstructed from that directory.
24	
25	## 1. The lane boundary
26	
27	Introduce these persistent records:
28	
29	```text
30	ResolveContext
31	  work_id
32	  contract_id
33	  identifiers[]        # DOI, PMCID, PMID, OAI ID, CELEX, ECLI, etc.
34	  title
35	  authors[]
36	  year
37	  required_artifact_kinds[]
38	  permitted_expression_kinds[]
39	  required_capabilities[]
40	
41	DocumentCandidate
42	  candidate_id
43	  work_id
44	  discovered_by_route
45	  resolver_request_id
46	  identifier_used
47	  retrieval_url
48	  repository_record_url
49	  media_hint
50	  version_hint          # observation only
51	  license_observation   # observation only
52	  raw_metadata_blob_id
53	  raw_metadata_hash
54	
55	Manifestation
56	  manifestation_id
57	  candidate_id
58	  retrieval_request_id
59	  raw_blob_id
60	  raw_hash
61	  extracted_text_blob_id
62	  extracted_text_hash
63	  extraction_version
64	```
65	
66	The adapter must not write `FULLTEXT`, `THIS_WORK`, `VERSION_OF_RECORD`, `ADMISSIBLE`, or an expression edge. Those are reducer outputs derived from immutable metadata and document bytes.
67	
68	`candidate_id` must follow the entire chain:
69	
70	```text
71	resolver request → candidate → content request → redirects → manifestation
72	```
73	
74	That fixes the current route-credit error: [source_router.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/source_router.py) can presently credit an adapter with a full-text manifestation fetched through some other adapter because it reduces all work-level manifestations rather than the adapter’s own candidate lineage.
75	
76	Also remove the flat-corpus write in [deep_fetch.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/deep_fetch.py:185):
77	
78	- It currently admits anything except `CITATION_ONLY`, including some unreadable/non-document results.
79	- It truncates extracted text to 120,000 characters.
80	- It discards the manifestation identity needed by the law.
81	
82	Downstream synthesis should receive verified binding IDs, never detached text files.
83	
84	## 2. Route specifications
85	
86	All “yield” figures are gross forecasts among the approximately 2,490 no-known-OA candidates. They overlap and are not additive.
87	
88	### PMC
89	
90	- Query:
91	  1. Convert DOI/PMID to PMCID with the PMC ID Converter.
92	  2. `GetRecord` from PMC OAI using `oai:pubmedcentral.nih.gov:<numeric-pmcid>` and `metadataPrefix=pmc`.
93	  3. If needed, query the PMC OA service for PDF/tarball links.
94	- Resolves by: PMCID first; DOI or PMID through ID conversion.
95	- Returns: JATS full-text XML where rights permit; the OA service may return PDF/package links. A PMCID does not guarantee downloadable OA full text.
96	- Politeness: PMC OAI allows at most three requests per second, no concurrent requests, and recommends large harvesting off-peak. Batch up to 200 IDs through the ID converter. Register tool/email. [PMC ID Converter](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/id-converter-api/), [PMC OAI](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/oai/), [PMC OA service](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/oa-service/), [NCBI usage guidance](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK25497/)
97	- Clinical: primary full-text route; prefer JATS because identity, sections, contributors, DOI, and closure are structurally observable.
98	- Legal/comparative: invoked only when the contract requests scholarly literature carrying DOI/PMID identifiers, not for official legal texts.
99	- Thin evidence: a rights-blocked or missing OAI record is a route observation, not proof that no document exists.
100	- Data edit: PMC endpoints, identifier transforms, metadata prefixes, and rate budget. Code changes only for a new protocol/parser primitive.
101	- Silent failures: PMCID exists but full text is not in the OA subset; XML is front matter only; NIH manuscript is mistaken for publisher VoR; embargo/live status is ignored.
102	- Gross yield forecast: 20–60.
103	
104	### Europe PMC
105	
106	- Query: exact DOI search using `/rest/search?query=DOI:"..."&resultType=core&format=json`; inspect `pmcid`, `fullTextIdList`, `fullTextUrlList`, `hasPDF`, and OA fields. Fetch JATS with `/rest/{PMCID}/fullTextXML`.
107	- Resolves by: DOI, PMCID, PMID, or Europe PMC source/id.
108	- Returns: core metadata, full-text URLs, and OA JATS XML for eligible records. The currently configured `/{source}/{id}/fullTextXML` shape is wrong for PMCID retrieval.
109	- Politeness: official documentation does not publish a stable numeric public quota. Start at one request/second and one in flight, then obey `429`, `Retry-After`, and any returned limit headers. [Europe PMC REST](https://europepmc.org/RestfulWebService), [official API specification](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/api/swagger.json)
110	- Clinical: complements PMC with European indexing and identifiers.
111	- Legal/comparative: only for relevant scholarly literature.
112	- Thin evidence: distinguishes “record found, no accessible full text” from “identifier not found.”
113	- Data edit: endpoint templates, response selectors, rate budget.
114	- Silent failures: abstract mistaken for full text; PMCID present but XML unavailable; source/id normalization error; duplicated PMC copy counted as incremental.
115	- Incremental gross yield forecast after PMC: 5–20.
116	
117	### DOAJ
118	
119	- Query: exact article search on DOI; read `bibjson.link[]` entries whose type is `fulltext`.
120	- Resolves by: DOI primarily; title plus author only as a candidate generator.
121	- Returns: metadata and publisher/repository full-text URLs, generally not document bytes.
122	- Politeness: average two requests/second; brief bursts may be queued. [DOAJ API](https://doaj.org/api/)
123	- Clinical: finds OA journals outside PMC coverage.
124	- Legal/comparative: finds OA legal and comparative scholarship, but never official legislation or judgments.
125	- Thin evidence: a DOAJ miss means “not indexed in DOAJ,” not “not open.”
126	- Data edit: query template and link selector.
127	- Silent failures: stale or landing-page URL; URL redirects to login; DOI metadata points to a supplement; directory membership is mistaken for byte-level version proof.
128	- Incremental gross yield forecast: 10–25.
129	
130	### CORE
131	
132	- Query: `/v3/search/works?q=doi:"..."`; prefer record `downloadUrl` or documented output download. Treat a returned `fullText` field as a derived text manifestation, not automatically as a complete PDF equivalent.
133	- Resolves by: DOI; CORE work/output ID; OAI ID; title plus author for unresolved candidates.
134	- Returns: metadata, extracted full text, source URLs, download URLs, and repository/OAI identifiers.
135	- Politeness: enforce the authenticated tier reported by CORE and its rate headers. Current documentation describes materially different daily and per-minute budgets by tier; full-text download is more expensive. The currently configured credential returns 401, so preflight must mark the route unavailable rather than conclude that content is absent. [CORE API](https://api.core.ac.uk/docs/v3)
136	- Clinical: useful for accepted manuscripts and institutional copies outside PMC.
137	- Legal/comparative: strong for repository scholarship and working papers, but repository copies are not official legal texts.
138	- Thin evidence: an exhausted authenticated exact-DOI query contributes to search coverage; an unauthenticated or 401 run contributes only `BACKEND_FAILED`.
139	- Data edit: credential profile, query template, output selectors, rate costs.
140	- Silent failures: OCR/truncated `fullText`; supplement returned as primary file; API tier omits full text; direct file link expires; repository metadata says accepted manuscript but bytes are another work.
141	- Gross yield forecast: 60–140.
142	
143	### OpenAIRE Graph
144	
145	- Query: current Graph API `/graph/v3/research-products?pid=<doi>`; inspect product instances, PIDs, access rights, licences, and URLs.
146	- Resolves by: DOI/PID or OpenAIRE research-product ID.
147	- Returns: graph metadata and candidate locations. URLs remain candidates; OpenAIRE metadata is not document proof.
148	- Politeness: current documented limits are up to 60 requests/hour unauthenticated and 7,200/hour authenticated. Use authentication and schedule against the lower of configured and observed limits. [OpenAIRE Graph API](https://graph.openaire.eu/docs/apis/graph-api/), [OpenAIRE API terms](https://graph.openaire.eu/docs/apis/terms)
149	- Clinical: finds institutional and national repository copies not exposed through PMC.
150	- Legal/comparative: finds cross-jurisdictional scholarship and repository deposits.
151	- Thin evidence: exact PID exhaustion can close this route; stale or inaccessible candidate URLs cannot.
152	- Data edit: replace the legacy endpoint in YAML with the Graph v3 operation and response selectors.
153	- Silent failures: access-right label is stale; URL is only a repository landing page; multiple instances represent the same file; graph metadata and fetched bytes disagree.
154	- Gross yield forecast: 45–110.
155	
156	### Zenodo
157	
158	- Query: `/api/records?q=doi:"..."`; also inspect `pids`, related identifiers, `conceptdoi`, version, resource type, and each file.
159	- Resolves by: record DOI, concept DOI, related DOI, Zenodo record ID; title plus creator only for candidate generation.
160	- Returns: record metadata and direct file links.
161	- Politeness: rate limits are endpoint/account dependent; use `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `Remaining`, and `Reset`, and defer on 429. [Zenodo API](https://developers.zenodo.org/)
162	- Clinical: can contain manuscripts, trial artifacts, protocols, data, and supplements. Artifact type must remain explicit.
163	- Legal/comparative: may contain scholarship or datasets, but not authoritative law merely because it has a DOI.
164	- Thin evidence: non-article files may themselves be the correct evidence artifact if the contract requested protocols or data; otherwise they do not fill the full-article role.
165	- Data edit: resource-type mappings, PID fields, file selectors, rate budget.
166	- Silent failures: dataset/supplement mistaken for article; concept DOI resolves to the wrong version; restricted record has metadata but no bytes; multiple files are concatenated into a fictitious document.
167	- Gross yield forecast: 5–20.
168	
169	### Institutional OAI-PMH
170	
171	- Query:
172	  - Do not attempt global per-work OAI harvesting.
173	  - Obtain the OAI identifier and repository base URL through CORE, OpenAIRE, repository metadata, or an incrementally maintained local index.
174	  - Use `GetRecord(identifier, metadataPrefix)`.
175	  - Rich formats such as JATS, METS, MODS, OAI-ORE, or repository-specific XML may expose document URLs. `oai_dc` commonly exposes metadata or a landing page only.
176	- Resolves by: OAI identifier. OAI-PMH has no universal exact-DOI lookup.
177	- Returns: metadata records; sometimes file URLs or embedded structured full text, depending on the repository and metadata format.
178	- Politeness: repository-specific. Default to one in flight and a two-second interval until the repository row states otherwise; obey `Retry-After`, robots rules, resumption tokens, and deletion records. [OAI-PMH specification](https://www.openarchives.org/OAI/openarchivesprotocol.html)
179	- Clinical: reaches university manuscripts and subject repositories beyond PMC.
180	- Legal/comparative: reaches institutional scholarship; official courts and legislatures should use dedicated official-source adapters.
181	- Thin evidence: only a clean `GetRecord`/indexed lookup closes that repository. A missing local DOI-to-OAI mapping does not.
182	- Data edit: each repository is a row containing base URL, supported metadata prefixes, identifier mappings, file XPath/selectors, and rate policy. A code edit is needed only for a genuinely new metadata dialect primitive.
183	- Silent failures: `oai_dc` landing page treated as PDF; deleted record treated as no-world evidence; DOI belongs to a cited reference; repository cover sheet hides a different article; resumption state is lost.
184	- Gross yield forecast: 35–90.
185	
186	## 3. Priority and stopping policy
187	
188	Given a DOI, resolve in waves:
189	
190	1. Re-derive identity, completeness, and admissibility for already held bytes.
191	2. In parallel, perform cheap exact-ID resolution through PMC/Europe PMC where applicable, DOAJ, Unpaywall, OpenAlex, and Semantic Scholar.
192	3. Resolve through CORE, OpenAIRE, and Zenodo.
193	4. Use resulting OAI IDs/repository locations for targeted institutional OAI-PMH.
194	5. Use title-plus-author only after exact identifiers fail. It may generate candidates but cannot establish identity.
195	
196	Candidate fetching is ordered separately:
197	
198	1. Official or authoritative structured full text permitted by the contract: PMC JATS, official legal XML, registry JSON.
199	2. Publisher-hosted OA VoR.
200	3. Repository-hosted bytes whose own front matter proves they are the VoR.
201	4. Accepted manuscript.
202	5. Preprint or working paper.
203	
204	A publisher URL gets one normal attempt. A 403 ends that URL attempt and advances to repositories; it does not trigger repeated publisher requests.
205	
206	Stop rules:
207	
208	- If a complete, identity-confirmed, policy-admissible manifestation is obtained, remaining lower-priority work is cancelled with a pointer to the reducer decision that satisfied the contract.
209	- If only an accepted manuscript is found under a journal-VoR-only contract, keep searching every applicable VoR route.
210	- If the contract permits accepted manuscripts, it can satisfy the role only as an accepted manuscript and must be attributed as such.
211	
212	## 4. Version equivalence
213	
214	`accepted_manuscript_of` remains a useful bibliographic edge, but it is not span-preserving.
215	
216	Change the attribution API from manifestation-wide permission to binding-specific permission:
217	
218	```text
219	resolve_attribution(binding_id, attribution_policy)
220	```
221	
222	A `SpanCorrespondence` must contain:
223	
224	```text
225	source_manifestation_id + raw/text hash + offsets + verbatim span
226	target_manifestation_id + raw/text hash + offsets + verbatim span
227	canonicalization algorithm and version
228	exact canonical-span hash
229	identity decisions for both manifestations
230	```
231	
232	The verifier rechecks both hashes, both offsets, and exact canonical text equality.
233	
234	Consequences:
235	
236	- Repository metadata alone can never prove span equivalence.
237	- A matching span found independently in VoR bytes should normally be rebound directly to the VoR manifestation.
238	- A correspondence grants permission only for that span, not every assertion in the manuscript.
239	- A disagreement such as 0.37 versus 0.2 fails immediately.
240	- Expression-wide `exact_copy_of` is reserved for identity-confirmed manifestations whose entire canonical document text is equal.
241	- An accepted manuscript without VoR bytes remains accepted-manuscript-attributable only.
242	
243	## 5. Wrong-work and completeness defence
244	
245	Identity extraction must use the fetched content itself:
246	
247	- JATS/XML: article DOI, title group, contributors, journal, publication dates.
248	- PDF: front-matter DOI, normalized title, byline, journal/version marks from the first pages. Repository cover sheets are segmented from article front matter.
249	- HTML: citation metadata plus visible article header. Metadata alone is insufficient if visible content conflicts.
250	- Legal text: package/CELEX/ECLI/docket/citation and revision/as-of markers.
251	- Registry: registry identifier and record version.
252	
253	Decision rules:
254	
255	- `CONFIRMED`: exact front-matter DOI, or high-specificity title plus compatible byline with no conflicting identifier.
256	- `DIFFERENT_WORK`: positive foreign DOI, or an incompatible foreign title plus disjoint byline.
257	- `UNRESOLVED`: generic title collision, weak title-only match, unreadable front matter, or contradictory metadata.
258	
259	A generic title without an author must not produce `DIFFERENT_WORK`; the current event reducer is too aggressive here.
260	
261	Completeness is artifact-specific and data-driven:
262	
263	- JATS article: body-bearing structure and terminal sections/closure.
264	- PDF article: valid page structure, readable terminal pages, no truncation/download-error evidence.
265	- Legal judgment: short documents can be complete; use official structural closure rather than word thresholds.
266	- Registry: required record fields and version envelope, not prose length.
267	
268	Every label is recomputed from raw content. HTTP 200, MIME type, API `fullText`, repository `acceptedVersion`, and filename are observations, never conclusions.
269	
270	## 6. Generality contract
271	
272	| Mechanism | Clinical | Legal/comparative | Thin evidence | Domain change |
273	|---|---|---|---|---|
274	| Capability routing | Selects trials, reviews, registries, guidelines, and biomedical full text | Selects official legislation/judgments plus doctrinal scholarship | Selects all routes capable of the required artifact, then reports clean exhaustion separately from failures | Add capability/artifact/source-policy rows |
275	| Identity | DOI/PMCID/NCT plus title/byline | CELEX/ECLI/package/docket/citation plus title/date | Leaves weak matches unresolved instead of forcing a corpus | Add identifier extractors/XPaths as data when supported |
276	| Version policy | Distinguishes registry, preprint, AM, and journal VoR | Distinguishes official revision, consolidated text, judgment, commentary | Allows “literature does not settle this” after admissible evidence is genuinely thin | Add permitted expression kinds to contract data |
277	| Completeness | JATS/article/registry profiles | Official XML/PDF and revision profiles | Short complete artifacts remain complete | Add artifact-profile rows |
278	| Search outcome | Separates no PMC full text from backend failure | Separates no official text from scholarship-only results | `SEARCHED_NONE`, `THIN`, and `SEARCH_FAILED` stay distinct | Add route coverage rows |
279	
280	The router’s primary input must therefore be structured contract capabilities, not lexical topic matching. YAML token triggers can remain only as a low-confidence fallback. There must be no `if domain == clinical` or legal-topic regex.
281	
282	Adding GovInfo, EUR-Lex, CourtListener, or another repository should normally mean adding rows for:
283	
284	- protocol/adapter kind;
285	- supported artifact and jurisdiction capabilities;
286	- exact identifiers;
287	- endpoint templates;
288	- response/file selectors;
289	- version/revision fields;
290	- rate policy;
291	- authority class.
292	
293	Code changes are warranted for a new wire protocol or a new reusable document/identity primitive—not for a new subject domain.
294	
295	## 7. Politeness scheduler
296	
297	Replace `_HOST_LAST` and the single `SPACING_S` in [acquisition.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/acquisition.py) with a cross-process persistent scheduler:
298	
299	- Per-host token bucket and concurrency limit.
300	- Resolver host and content host budgeted separately; redirects charge the destination host.
301	- Configured limits enforced from `source_routes.yaml`.
302	- Dynamic limits can only reduce the configured rate unless explicitly approved.
303	- Parse both numeric and HTTP-date `Retry-After`.
304	- Store `not_before`; do not sleep and retry three seconds later when the server requests hours.
305	- Cache exact-ID resolver responses; use ETag/Last-Modified where supported.
306	- Batch PMC identifiers.
307	- Circuit-break on repeated 401/403/429/5xx.
308	- Parallelize across independent hosts, not within a host beyond its policy.
309	
310	Outcome semantics:
311	
312	- 429 → `THROTTLED` and deferred.
313	- 401 → `AUTH_FAILED`.
314	- 403 → `ACCESS_DENIED` for that URL.
315	- Timeout/5xx → bounded retry then `BACKEND_FAILED`.
316	- None of these may reduce to “no OA copy exists.”
317	
318	## 8. Yield forecast
319	
320	For the approximately 2,490 candidates with no known OA URL:
321	
322	- Complete, correct-work document manifestations: approximately 450–850, or 18–34%.
323	- Strict journal-VoR-attributable full texts: approximately 180–360, or 7–14%.
324	- Additional accepted-manuscript/preprint leads: approximately 200–400, not counted as strict journal full text.
325	
326	The best working point estimate is roughly 260 additional strict journal-attributable full texts. It is not yet measured strongly enough to use as a commitment.
327	
328	Calibrate with a frozen stratified canary of 200–300 DOI-bearing candidates, split across venue/year/field/access status. Report unique incremental yield after each route, not gross candidates.
329	
330	## 9. Build and release order
331	
332	1. Close the accepted-manuscript P0 and route-lineage bug.
333	2. Add candidate/provenance schema and persistent host scheduler.
334	3. Wire PMC, Europe PMC, and DOAJ.
335	4. Add CORE, OpenAIRE Graph v3, and Zenodo.
336	5. Add generic targeted OAI-PMH.
337	6. Add byte-derived identity, version, completeness, and per-span correspondence.
338	7. Run a cumulative ladder on one frozen corpus, one route addition at a time.
339	8. Only then freeze the enriched corpus and run synthesis comparisons.
340	
341	Required tests:
342	
343	- PMC VoR JATS becomes admissible.
344	- NIH/accepted manuscript remains non-VoR.
345	- The Parry/Yang-Hui He wrong-work case is quarantined.
346	- The Acemoglu 0.37/0.2 mismatch cannot align.
347	- An independently matching VoR span can be rebound.
348	- A short legal judgment is recognized as complete.
349	- A complete registry record does not need article-length prose.
350	- 429 never becomes `SEARCHED_NONE`.
351	- One route cannot inherit another route’s manifestation.
352	- Multiple worker processes remain inside the host budget.
353	- Truncated or HTML-error downloads cannot enter synthesis.
354	
355	For every ladder step, report attempted, responded, candidate URLs, fetched manifestations, complete documents, identity-confirmed/unresolved/different-work, expression versions, strict journal-attributable count, unique incremental yield, overlap, 401/403/429, and unresolved route failures. Criterion-level evaluation must follow on a clinical, legal/comparative, and thin-evidence holdout before calling the lane general.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""TYPED WORK / EXPRESSION / MANIFESTATION GRAPH — attribution may name only what the bytes prove.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	
6	  The corpus was one row per DOI. One row means one identity, so the row for a QJE article and the
7	  bytes of an NBER working paper were THE SAME OBJECT. `wp_fetch.py` wrote the working paper's text
8	  into `row['fulltext']`; the row kept `attribution = "Writing in the Quarterly Journal of Economics
9	  in 2003, Autor, Levy and Murnane"`. The miner then bound spans to that row, the writer named the
10	  journal, and the gate checked the sentence against the working paper's span. Every component was
11	  honest. The DATA MODEL did the lying: it had nowhere to put the difference.
12	
13	  Sol, verbatim: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but
14	  they are DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven. The recovered text is presently A
15	  DISCOVERY LEAD, not automatically journal-attributable evidence."
16	
17	  So the row is split into three typed nodes, and the difference gets somewhere to live:
18	
19	      Work           the research object / case / trial          "what was studied"
20	        Expression   the version of the report of that work      "which version says it"
21	          Manifest.  the retrieved bytes + content hash          "the text we actually hold"
22	
23	  Spans bind to a MANIFESTATION, never to a Work. That is the whole point: `span -> manifestation`
24	  is a fact about bytes we hold; `span -> work` is a wish.
25	
26	THE CORE RULE (Sol, verbatim)
27	
28	  "Every span binds to its exact manifestation_id and content hash. ATTRIBUTION MAY NAME ONLY THAT
29	   MANIFESTATION, unless a stronger edge proves that the cited source contains the same span."
30	
31	  Implemented in `Graph.attribution_targets()`. An edge only widens what a span may name if it is
32	  SPAN-PRESERVING (exact_copy_of, authenticated accepted_manuscript_of) *and* ASSERTED. A
33	  `predecessor_of` edge never widens anything, however plausible: PEER REVIEW CHANGES NUMBERS.
34	
35	  "A title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT exact_copy_of."
36	  -> every edge carries `status` (PROPOSED | ASSERTED) and a `basis` naming the evidence. An edge
37	     with no basis cannot be constructed.
38	
39	NO UNIVERSAL WORD THRESHOLD (Sol, item 4)
40	
41	  "A universal word threshold cannot define 'full text': a short judicial opinion, statute section,
42	   trial-registry record, and journal article have different completeness profiles."
43	
44	  MIN_WORDS=2500 was my own invention and it is deleted. `profile()` derives, FROM THE BYTES:
45	  artifact_kind, sections present, body/chrome ratio, result-bearing sections, extractability,
46	  identity agreement, fetch outcome — and judges completeness AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR THAT KIND.
47	  A four-page judicial opinion is COMPLETE. A 535-word cookie banner is not, at any length.
48	
49	  There is exactly ONE reducer that may answer "is this the whole document?": `judge_completeness()`,
50	  driven by the KIND_PROFILE registry. `profile()` calls it, `Graph.from_json()` calls it, and
51	  `event_ledger.derive_content_profile()` calls it. It was written twice before — 1,200 words here
52	  and FULLTEXT_MIN=2,500 there — which is not a shared rule, it is two rules that disagree, and the
53	  document a card cites depends on which module last looked at it.
54	
55	THE API. EXACTLY THREE ENTRY POINTS (Sol, "reality checks", item 5)
56	
57	    bind_span(manifestation_id, start, end)   -> bytes -> a bound span. BOUNDS ARE VALIDATED.
58	    resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, policy) -> what that span may NAME, under THIS task's rule
59	    verify_span(binding)                      -> the enforcement point re-checks EVERYTHING
60	
61	  bind_span() returns IDENTIFIERS — `expression_id` and `permitted_expression_ids`. It used to return
62	  `may_name`: a list of English SENTENCES. A sentence is a display cache, and a consumer that must
63	  string-match prose to decide what is legal has no rule at all — it has a suggestion. Prose is
64	  produced by resolve_attribution(), carried as `.text`, and NOTHING IS EVER VALIDATED AGAINST IT.
65	
66	WHAT RE-DERIVING THE LABELS FROM THE BYTES ACTUALLY FOUND
67	
68	  The memo assumed the six `fulltext_source='working_paper'` rows were working papers. The bytes say
69	  that label recorded WHICH FETCHER RAN, not WHAT THE ARTIFACT IS. It is false in both directions:
70	
71	    Parry et al. (2016), Group & Organization Management, "Rise of the Machines"
72	        -> the bytes are Yang-Hui He, "MATHEMATICS: THE RISE OF THE MACHINES", an arXiv PAPER ABOUT
73	           THEOREM-PROVING. None of Parry, Cohen or Bhattacharya appears anywhere in the 4,027 words.
74	           The title matcher matched the phrase "Rise of the Machines". This is not a predecessor,
75	           not a preprint, not a version — IT IS A DIFFERENT WORK, and 17 of its numbers were queued
76	           to be printed as findings of a management journal.
77	    Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019), JEP -> the bytes ARE the typeset JEP article. Not a working paper.
78	    Autor (2015), JEP                 -> the bytes are the AEA WEBSITE'S COOKIE BANNER. 535 words.
79	    Autor et al. (2003), QJE          -> readable body, but chars 0-19,569 are (cid:N) font codes and
80	                                         NOTHING in the bytes says whether this is QJE or NBER 8337.
81	                                         Genuinely UNRESOLVED -> therefore NOT journal-attributable.
82	
83	  And one the memo never suspected, found by the same content check, ALREADY SHIPPED IN A CARD:
84	
85	    Weiss (2008), Economics Letters   -> the PDF extracted as a CAESAR-SHIFTED GLYPH DUMP.
86	        span:  "...wkh qxpehu ri kdluguhvvhuv juhz pruh wkdq 633 shu fhqw ehwzhhq 4<<5 dqg 4<<<"
87	        plain: "...the number of hairdressers grew more than 300 per cent between 1992 and 1999"
88	        card:  "633 percent increase in number of hairdressers"          THE PAPER SAYS 300.
89	
90	        The span gate did its job perfectly: that span IS verbatim in the source. But the SOURCE was
91	        a corrupted encoding of the paper, so "verbatim from the source" was verbatim from garbage.
92	        EXTRACTION QUALITY IS A PRECONDITION FOR THE GATE TO MEAN ANYTHING, and nothing was checking
93	        it. That is why `extractability` is in the profile and why it is load-bearing for
94	        faithfulness, not merely for tidy labelling.
95	
96	    usage:  python3 scripts/provenance.py            # the honest census
97	"""
98	from __future__ import annotations
99	
100	import hashlib
101	import json
102	import re
103	import statistics
104	import sys
105	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
106	from pathlib import Path
107	
108	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
109	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
110	
111	# ONE definition of "section", shared with the miner. Two functions that each decide what a section
112	# is will drift, and every offset stored in every card indexes into whichever one won that day.
113	from evidence_miner import View, sections_of, SECTION_WEIGHT  # noqa: E402
114	
115	CORPUS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
116	GRAPH_OUT = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'provenance_graph.json'
117	
118	#: THE API. A span becomes a citation through these three functions and no others.
119	#:     bind_span(manifestation_id, start, end)        -> Graph.bind_span
120	#:     resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, policy)  -> Graph.resolve_attribution
121	#:     verify_span(binding)                           -> Graph.verify_span
122	__all__ = [
123	    'Graph', 'Work', 'Expression', 'Manifestation', 'Edge',
124	    'SourcePolicy', 'Attribution', 'SpanBindingError', 'GraphIntegrityError',
125	    'JOURNAL_ONLY', 'PEER_REVIEWED', 'OFFICIAL_TEXT', 'ANY_VERSION',
126	    'KIND_PROFILE', 'ARTIFACT_KINDS', 'FINDING_BEARING_KINDS', 'EXPRESSION_KINDS', 'WORK_KINDS',
127	    'judge_completeness', 'profile', 'derive_expression_kind', 'derive_source_type', 'migrate',
128	]
129	
130	
131	# =================================================================================================
132	# 1. NODE TYPES
133	# =================================================================================================
134	
135	# The version taxonomy. `repository` is NOT a version — a repository copy is a MANIFESTATION (a place
136	# bytes live) of some expression, which is where it sits in the graph below.
137	#
138	# A JUDICIAL OPINION HAS NO `journal_version`, AND A STATUTE HAS NO PREPRINT. The scholarly five were
139	# the only kinds here, and `migrate()` therefore minted a `journal_version` for every row it saw —
140	# which is not a taxonomy, it is an assumption wearing one. `official_text` and `registry_record` are
141	# the authoritative expressions of the non-scholarly works, and the registry below says which work
142	# kind has which.
143	EXPRESSION_KINDS = ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version', 'accepted_manuscript', 'working_paper',
144	                    'preprint', 'official_text', 'registry_record', 'unknown')
145	
146	
147	@dataclass
148	class Work:
149	    """The research object / case / trial. NOT a document. Nothing binds to it.
150	
151	    `kind` IS LOAD-BEARING, not decoration: it selects the completeness profile its documents are
152	    judged against. A `case` is judged as a judicial opinion — COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH — and a `study`
153	    is judged as a scholarly article, where 300 words is a fragment. Judge one by the other's rule and
154	    you either destroy the opinion or admit the fragment.
155	    """
156	    id: str
157	    title: str
158	    authors: list[str]
159	    year: int | None
160	    venue: str | None            # the venue / court / reporter / registry that names the official text
161	    doi: str | None
162	    kind: str = 'study'          # WORK_KINDS
163	
164	
165	@dataclass
166	class Expression:
167	    """A VERSION of the report of a Work. This is the unit an attribution may name.
168	
169	    There is NO `citable_in_journal_only_answer` field any more. Citability is a property of THE
170	    ANSWER'S INSTRUCTION, not of the node: the same accepted manuscript is inadmissible under
171	    "journal articles only" and admissible under "any identified version". A boolean stored on the
172	    node has to be wrong for one of them, and a stored label that can be wrong is this whole file's
173	    subject. It is DERIVED, per policy, by `Graph.resolve_attribution()`.
174	    """
175	    id: str
176	    work_id: str
177	    kind: str                     # EXPRESSION_KINDS
178	    kind_basis: str               # WHY we believe that — derived from bytes, or 'claimed_by_metadata'
179	    attribution: str              # DISPLAY ONLY. The sentence a writer may render for THIS expression.
180	    #                             # Nothing is ever validated against it — see THE LAW.
181	
182	
183	@dataclass
184	class Manifestation:
185	    """The BYTES WE ACTUALLY HOLD. Every span binds here, and to `content_hash`."""
186	    id: str
187	    expression_id: str
188	    work_id: str
189	    text: str                     # retained in full. NOTHING IS EVER DELETED.
190	    content_hash: str             # sha256 of `text`
191	    n_words: int
192	    locator: str | None           # the URL these bytes came from
193	    locator_status: str           # RECORDED | NOT_RECORDED_BY_FETCHER | CONTRADICTS_CONTENT
194	    fetched_by: str               # which fetcher wrote it
195	    text_field: str               # which corpus field it came from ('fulltext' | 'abstract')
196	    profile: dict = field(default_factory=dict)   # the content-derived artifact profile
197	
198	
199	@dataclass
200	class Edge:
201	    """A TYPED, EVIDENCED relation. An edge with no basis cannot be constructed."""
202	    src: str
203	    dst: str
204	    type: str                     # EDGE_TYPES
205	    status: str                   # PROPOSED | ASSERTED
206	    basis: str                    # the evidence. Never empty.
207	
208	
209	EDGE_TYPES = ('exact_copy_of', 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'predecessor_of',
210	              'reports_same_study', 'supersedes', 'cites')
211	
212	# THE ONLY EDGES THAT MAY WIDEN AN ATTRIBUTION.
213	# A span in manifestation M may name expression E != M.expression ONLY across one of these, and ONLY
214	# when the edge is ASSERTED. `predecessor_of` and `reports_same_study` are deliberately absent:
215	# a working paper and the journal article report the same study, and PEER REVIEW CHANGES NUMBERS.
216	SPAN_PRESERVING = ('exact_copy_of', 'accepted_manuscript_of')
217	
218	
219	# =================================================================================================
220	# 2. CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE  — this replaces MIN_WORDS
221	# =================================================================================================
222	
223	# --- extractability ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
224	# Thresholds are DERIVED FROM THE OBSERVED DISTRIBUTION of this corpus, not invented, and __main__
225	# reprints that distribution so they can be re-derived by anyone who doubts them. On the 70-row
226	# corpus the stopword density of intact prose runs 0.198 - 0.45 (median 0.292); the single
227	# Caesar-shifted document sits at 0.012. The gap is 16x and the threshold below falls in empty space.
228	_FUNCTION_WORDS = frozenset("""the of and to in a is that for with as on by are we this be it from at
229	an not which or have has was were their its they there than these such been between more can may""".split())
230	CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY = 0.10   # < this => the bytes are not English prose (cipher / glyph dump)
231	DEGRADED_CID_RATIO = 0.05         # > this => a non-embedded font left (cid:N) codes in the text
232	
233	_CID = re.compile(r'\(cid:\d+\)')
234	_WORDY = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z']+")
235	
236	# --- chrome --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
237	# Web furniture. A landing page is not a paper at ANY word count, and 548 words of it is not an
238	# "abstract" either -- calling it one implies the paper's own summary, which we do not have.
239	_CHROME = re.compile(
240	    r'(uses cookies|cookie (policy|settings|consent)|accept(ing)? (all )?cookies|skip to (main|content)'
241	    r'|sign in|log ?in|register|create an account|privacy policy|terms (of use|and conditions)'
242	    r'|newsletter|subscribe|add to cart|purchase|download pdf|share (this|on)|permissions'
243	    r'|all rights reserved|advertisement|search this site|contact name email|send me)', re.I)
244	
245	# --- self-identifying version furniture ----------------------------------------------------------
246	_WP_MARK = re.compile(
247	    r'(nber working paper|national bureau of economic research|working paper (no\.?|series|\d)'
248	    r'|iza (discussion paper|dp)|discussion paper (no\.?|series)|cepr discussion paper'
249	    r'|preliminary(?: and incomplete)?[.:—-]? *(?:please )?do not (cite|quote)'
250	    r'|comments? welcome|this draft)', re.I)
251	_PREPRINT_MARK = re.compile(r'(arxiv[:\s]*\d{4}\.\d{4,5}|ssrn[- ]id|preprint (submitted|version)|biorxiv)', re.I)
252	_AM_MARK = re.compile(
253	    r"(accepted manuscript|author'?s? (accepted|final) (version|manuscript)|postprint"
254	    r'|published version\)? *\(refereed\)|this is the (author|accepted))', re.I)
255	# Typeset journal furniture: a running head with volume/issue/pages, or a publisher rights block.
256	_JOURNAL_MARK = re.compile(
257	    r'(volume \d+[,—-] *number \d+|vol\.? *\d+[,(]? *(no\.?|issue|\()? *\d+'
258	    r'|doi[:\s]*10\.\d{4,9}/|©\s*(the author|\d{4})|article reuse guidelines'
259	    r'|received .{0,40}(revised|accepted).{0,40}\d{4})', re.I)
260	
261	# =================================================================================================
262	# THE REGISTRY — the ONE table that says what each artifact kind IS and when it is COMPLETE.
263	# =================================================================================================
264	# Sol's point, made mechanical: completeness is a property OF A KIND, never of a word count.
265	#
266	# `stub_floor` separates THE DOCUMENT from A STUB OF IT, and it exists ONLY for the scholarly kinds,
267	# where a 500-word artifact is definitionally not the article (it is its abstract, or its landing
268	# page). For a judicial opinion, a statute section or a trial-registry record it is None: THOSE
269	# ARTIFACTS ARE COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. That is the whole difference between this and MIN_WORDS=2500,
270	# which asserted one number over every kind of document in the world.
271	#
272	# `expression` is the expression kind these bytes CONSTITUTE when they are the document — the link
273	# between "what is this artifact" and "what may a span in it name". A judicial opinion's bytes are its
274	# `official_text`; there is no journal version of it to widen to, ever.
275	#
276	# `needs_results` IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT. I first wrote it as a completeness gate and it declared six
277	# genuine journal articles incomplete — Technovation (15,985w), JEP (11,872w), AER (9,732w), JBR,
278	# Raj, Chalmers — because economics papers title their results sections after their CONTENT ("V. The
279	# Effect of Robots on Employment"), and a JEP essay has no results section at all. Gating on a
280	# section heading is the same error as gating on a word count, moved one axis over. The
281	# result-bearing sections are REPORTED in the profile (the miner weights by them) and GATE NOTHING.
282	
283	SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR = 1200     # scholarly kinds ONLY. It is not a fact about documents in general.
284	SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR = 120  # ...and below THIS, a scholarly fragment is a citation stub.
285	
286	KIND_PROFILE: dict[str, dict] = {
287	    'journal_article':       dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
288	                                  expression='journal_version',     finding_bearing=True),
289	    'proceedings_paper':     dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
290	                                  expression='proceedings_version', finding_bearing=True),
291	    'working_paper':         dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
292	                                  expression='working_paper',       finding_bearing=True),
293	    'preprint':              dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
294	                                  expression='preprint',            finding_bearing=True),
295	    'accepted_manuscript':   dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
296	                                  expression='accepted_manuscript', finding_bearing=True),
297	    # A short official opinion IS the whole artifact. No results section, no methods, and COMPLETE.
298	    # stub_floor=None IS THE POINT OF THIS TABLE. Do not put a number here. Ever.
299	    'judicial_opinion':      dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None,
300	                                  expression='official_text',   finding_bearing=True),
301	    'statute_section':       dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None,
302	                                  expression='official_text',   finding_bearing=True),
303	    'trial_registry_record': dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None,
304	                                  expression='registry_record', finding_bearing=True),
305	    # These are NEVER complete, at any length, and may never carry a finding. They have no expression:
306	    # a cookie banner is not a VERSION of the paper, it is not the paper at all.
307	    'abstract':              dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
308	    'citation_only':         dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
309	    'landing_page':          dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
310	    'extraction_failure':    dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
311	    'wrong_work':            dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
312	    'unknown':               dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None, expression=None, finding_bearing=False),
313	}
314	# DERIVED from the registry, never maintained beside it — two lists of kinds will drift, and the one
315	# that drifts is always the one a gate reads.
316	ARTIFACT_KINDS = tuple(KIND_PROFILE)
317	FINDING_BEARING_KINDS = tuple(k for k, v in KIND_PROFILE.items() if v['finding_bearing'])
318	
319	#: NOT A DOCUMENT AT ALL — as distinct from `unknown`, which IS a document whose VERSION we cannot
320	#: establish. Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) is 10,902 readable words whose title page is (cid:N) font
321	#: codes: we HOLD a document and cannot say which version it is. A cookie banner is not a document.
322	#: Reporting those two in one bucket would say "we hold nothing" about a paper we hold in full, which
323	#: is a different claim, and a false one.
324	NOT_A_DOCUMENT_KINDS = tuple(k for k, v in KIND_PROFILE.items()
325	                             if not v['finding_bearing'] and k != 'unknown')
326	
327	#: WHICH COMPLETENESS PROFILE DOES THIS WORK'S DOCUMENT GET? `None` => the scholarly family, whose
328	#: artifact kind is derived from the bytes' own version furniture. Everything else is fixed by the
329	#: work: a case produces judicial opinions, and no amount of NBER letterhead makes one a working paper.
330	WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT: dict[str, str | None] = {
331	    'study':   None,
332	    'case':    'judicial_opinion',
333	    'statute': 'statute_section',
334	    'trial':   'trial_registry_record',
335	}
336	WORK_KINDS = tuple(WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT)
337	
338	#: THE ROW'S OWN DECLARED TYPE -> (work kind, the expression the METADATA claims exists).
339	#: `migrate()` used to hardcode a `journal_version` for every row in the corpus. Sol: "That is not
340	#: valid for a judicial opinion or statute." The row DECLARES its type — every one of the 70 rows here
341	#: carries `type: 'journal-article'` from Crossref, and the code never once read it. It assumed the
342	#: answer it happened to be right about, which is not the same as being right.
343	SOURCE_TYPE: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
344	    'journal-article':     ('study',   'journal_version'),
345	    'journal article':     ('study',   'journal_version'),
346	    'proceedings-article': ('study',   'proceedings_version'),
347	    'book-chapter':        ('study',   'journal_version'),
348	    'posted-content':      ('study',   'preprint'),      # a preprint row claims NO journal version
349	    'preprint':            ('study',   'preprint'),
350	    'report':              ('study',   'working_paper'),
351	    'working-paper':       ('study',   'working_paper'),
352	    'judicial-opinion':    ('case',    'official_text'),
353	    'opinion':             ('case',    'official_text'),
354	    'case':                ('case',    'official_text'),
355	    'statute':             ('statute', 'official_text'),
356	    'legislation':         ('statute', 'official_text'),
357	    'clinical-trial':      ('trial',   'registry_record'),
358	    'trial-registration':  ('trial',   'registry_record'),
359	}
360	
361	RESULT_BEARING = ('results', 'tables', 'analysis')
362	
363	
364	def judge_completeness(artifact_kind: str, n_words: int,
365	                       extraction_verdict: str = 'CLEAN') -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
366	    """THE ONE COMPLETENESS REDUCER. `profile()`, `Graph.from_json()` and
367	    `event_ledger.derive_content_profile()` all come through here and through nothing else.
368	
369	    THERE IS NO UNIVERSAL WORD THRESHOLD IN THIS FUNCTION AND THERE MUST NEVER BE ONE. Completeness is
370	    read out of KIND_PROFILE for THIS kind: a judicial opinion, a statute section and a trial-registry
371	    record have stub_floor=None and are COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. A cookie banner is incomplete at ANY
372	    length, because `landing_page` cannot bear a finding at all — which is the correct reason, and not
373	    a fact about its size.
374	
375	    The one length that is never enough is ZERO. "Complete at any length" is not complete at no length:
376	    a registry record we hold nothing of is not a short registry record.
377	    """
378	    spec = KIND_PROFILE.get(artifact_kind)
379	    if spec is None:
380	        raise ValueError(f'{artifact_kind!r} is not a registered artifact kind — an unregistered kind '
381	                         f'cannot be judged complete OR incomplete, and defaulting it to either is how '
382	                         f'a label outruns its evidence. Register it in KIND_PROFILE.')
383	    reasons: list[str] = []
384	    if not spec['finding_bearing']:
385	        reasons.append(f'artifact kind `{artifact_kind}` cannot carry a finding')
386	    if extraction_verdict == 'CORRUPT':
387	        reasons.append('bytes are not readable prose')
388	    if n_words <= 0:
389	        reasons.append('there are no bytes — complete at any length is not complete at NO length')
390	    elif spec['stub_floor'] is not None and n_words < spec['stub_floor']:
391	        reasons.append(f'{n_words} words is below the stub floor for `{artifact_kind}` '
392	                       f'({spec["stub_floor"]}) — this is a fragment of the document, not the document')
393	    return (not reasons), reasons
394	
395	
396	def _norm(s: str) -> str:
397	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', (s or '')).strip()
398	
399	
400	def extractability(text: str) -> dict:
401	    """Are these bytes even a readable rendering of a document? Derived, never asserted."""
402	    toks = _WORDY.findall(text.lower())
403	    n = len(toks) or 1
404	    sw = sum(1 for t in toks if t in _FUNCTION_WORDS) / n
405	    words = text.split() or ['']
406	    cid = len(_CID.findall(text)) / len(words)
407	    alpha = sum(c.isalpha() or c.isspace() for c in text) / max(1, len(text))
408	    if sw < CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY:
409	        verdict = 'CORRUPT'          # not English. A cipher, a glyph dump, or not prose at all.
410	    elif cid > DEGRADED_CID_RATIO:
411	        verdict = 'DEGRADED'         # readable body, but a non-embedded font ate part of it
412	    else:
413	        verdict = 'CLEAN'
414	    return dict(verdict=verdict, stopword_density=round(sw, 4),
415	                cid_ratio=round(cid, 4), alpha_ratio=round(alpha, 3))
416	
417	
418	# A body that is the paper CONTAINS THE PAPER'S OWN ABSTRACT'S VOCABULARY -- the abstract is a
419	# summary of it. Measured on this corpus: every genuine body scores 0.975-1.000 (7 of 9 at exactly
420	# 1.000). The one body that is a DIFFERENT PAPER scores 0.391. The threshold below sits in the middle
421	# of that empty gap; __main__ reprints the distribution so it can be re-derived rather than believed.
422	CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP = 0.70
423	_IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS = 40      # below this the abstract is too thin to be a probe
424	_IDENT_MIN_VOCAB = 10
425	_IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS = 1000        # below this the body is a stub; absence proves nothing
426	
427	
428	def identity_agreement(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
429	                       readable: bool = True) -> dict:
430	    """Do these bytes belong to the Work the row claims? THE CHECK THAT CATCHES A DIFFERENT PAPER.
431	
432	    Not one component asked this. A title matcher matched the phrase "Rise of the Machines" and
433	    handed us a MATHEMATICS PREPRINT; every downstream check then verified, correctly and
434	    irrelevantly, that the span was verbatim IN THE MATHEMATICS PREPRINT.
435	
436	    CONTRADICTED REQUIRES POSITIVE EVIDENCE OF A DIFFERENT WORK. It is never inferred from the
437	    ABSENCE of ours. My first version of this function inferred it from absence -- "none of the
438	    claimed authors appears in the text" -- and it convicted EIGHT papers, all innocent: seven were
439	    bare abstracts that simply do not carry an author block, and the eighth was Autor (2003), whose
440	    title page is (cid:N) font codes. Meanwhile the ONE genuinely foreign paper scored CONFIRMED,
441	    because its two-word title ("Rise of the Machines") appears verbatim in the maths paper.
442	    Absence of our evidence, read as evidence of absence, is the HTTP-429-means-"no free copy exists"
443	    error exactly. So the only route to CONTRADICTED is a positive, two-sided content test:
444	
445	        the row carries an abstract FROM THE BIBLIOGRAPHY (independent of whatever we fetched);
446	        the body we fetched does NOT contain that abstract's distinctive vocabulary;
447	        therefore the body is not a rendering of this work.
448	    """
449	    head = text[:20000]
450	    authors = [a for a in (work.authors or []) if len(a) > 2]
451	    named = [a for a in authors if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(a) + r'\b', head, re.I)]
452	    twords = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (work.title or '').lower())}
453	    tfound = {w for w in twords if w in head.lower()}
454	    # A title of one or two distinctive words is not a test: "Rise of the Machines" yields {machines},
455	    # which the maths paper contains. Below three, the title signal is UNUSABLE, not passing.
456	    title_usable = len(twords) >= 3
457	
458	    overlap: float | None = None
459	    vocab = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (independent_abstract or '').lower())
460	             if w not in _FUNCTION_WORDS}
461	    if (len((independent_abstract or '').split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS
462	            and len(vocab) >= _IDENT_MIN_VOCAB
463	            and len(text.split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS):
464	        low = text.lower()
465	        overlap = round(sum(1 for w in vocab if w in low) / len(vocab), 3)
466	
467	    if not readable:
468	        # You cannot judge identity from bytes you cannot read. Autor (2003)'s authors and title are
469	        # absent because they are (cid:11)(cid:3)... -- that is a fact about the FONT, not the paper.
470	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'bytes are not readable — identity cannot be judged'
471	    elif overlap is not None and overlap < CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP:
472	        verdict = 'CONTRADICTED'
473	        basis = (f'the body contains only {overlap:.0%} of the distinctive vocabulary of this work’s '
474	                 f'own abstract (genuine bodies on this corpus: 97.5–100%) — THESE ARE NOT ITS BYTES')
475	    elif named:
476	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(named)}/{len(authors)} claimed authors named in the text'
477	    elif overlap is not None and overlap >= 0.90:
478	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'body contains {overlap:.0%} of its own abstract’s vocabulary'
479	    elif title_usable and len(tfound) >= 0.8 * len(twords):
480	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(tfound)}/{len(twords)} distinctive title words present'
481	    else:
482	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'no positive evidence either way (absence proves nothing)'
483	
484	    return dict(verdict=verdict, basis=basis,
485	                authors_named=len(named), authors_claimed=len(authors),
486	                title_words_found=len(tfound), title_words=len(twords), title_usable=title_usable,
487	                abstract_vocab_overlap=overlap)
488	
489	
490	def derive_expression_kind(text: str, work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, str]:
491	    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? Derived from self-identifying furniture.
492	
493	    Returns ('unknown', ...) freely. `unknown` is the honest answer for a PDF body with its title
494	    page mangled, and an honest `unknown` costs us evidence -- which is the correct price.
495	
496	    THE SCHOLARLY VERSION TAXONOMY APPLIES ONLY TO SCHOLARLY WORKS. There is no preprint of a statute
497	    and no accepted manuscript of a judicial opinion; the authoritative text IS the artifact, and the
498	    registry says so. Running the NBER/arXiv/"accepted manuscript" regexes over a court opinion does
499	    not find nothing — it finds whatever the opinion happens to QUOTE, which is how a whole-text scan
500	    for "NBER Working Paper" once read the bibliography and called the JEP article a working paper.
501	    """
502	    fam = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
503	    if fam is not None:
504	        ex = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']
505	        return ex, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is '
506	                    f'not the scholarly one; these bytes are its {ex.replace("_", " ")}')
507	    head = text[:12000]
508	    wp, pre, am, jr = (_WP_MARK.search(head), _PREPRINT_MARK.search(head),
509	                       _AM_MARK.search(head), _JOURNAL_MARK.search(head))
510	    # Order matters: an accepted manuscript deposited in a repository carries the journal's citation
511	    # block too, so the AM marker must be read BEFORE the journal marker or every AM reads as typeset.
512	    if am:
513	        return 'accepted_manuscript', f'bytes say: {am.group(0)[:48]!r}'
514	    if wp:
515	        return 'working_paper', f'bytes say: {wp.group(0)[:48]!r}'
516	    if pre:
517	        return 'preprint', f'bytes say: {pre.group(0)[:48]!r}'
518	    if jr:
519	        return 'journal_version', f'typeset journal furniture: {jr.group(0)[:48]!r}'
520	    return 'unknown', 'no self-identifying version furniture in the first 12,000 chars'
521	
522	
523	def profile(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
524	            fetch_outcome: str = 'ok') -> dict:
525	    """THE CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE. Replaces MIN_WORDS entirely.
526	
527	    Every field is derived FROM THE BYTES. `complete` is judged against the profile FOR THIS KIND,
528	    so a short judicial opinion is complete and a 535-word cookie banner is not.
529	    """
530	    text = text or ''
531	    words = len(text.split())
532	    ex = extractability(text)
533	    ident = identity_agreement(text, work, independent_abstract, readable=ex['verdict'] == 'CLEAN')
534	
535	    view = View(text)
536	    secs = sections_of(view)
537	    present = sorted({name for _, _, name in secs if name not in ('front', 'body')})
538	    result_secs = [s for s in present if s in RESULT_BEARING]   # REPORTED. GATES NOTHING. See above.
539	    # body/chrome ratio, measured on the bytes -- not on a word count.
540	    chrome_hits = len(_CHROME.findall(text))
541	    body_chars = sum(e - s for s, e, name in secs if SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5) > 0)
542	    body_ratio = round(body_chars / max(1, len(text)), 3)
543	    chrome_per_kw = round(1000 * chrome_hits / max(1, words), 2)
544	
545	    work_kind = work.kind or 'study'
546	    family = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind)      # None => scholarly; else the kind is FIXED by the work
547	    ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(text, work_kind=work_kind)
548	
549	    # ---- artifact_kind: DERIVED, in strict order of what the bytes can actually PROVE ------------
550	    # THE BYTES MAY ALWAYS DEMOTE. They may never promote. A judicial opinion whose fetch returned a
551	    # login wall is a landing_page, not a short opinion — so the demotions run FIRST, above the work's
552	    # own kind. But nothing in a stranger's letterhead can turn a statute into a working paper, so the
553	    # work's kind runs ABOVE the scholarly version furniture.
554	    if fetch_outcome != 'ok':
555	        # A failed fetch is a fact about our REQUEST, never a fact about the world.
556	        kind, basis = 'unknown', f'fetch outcome: {fetch_outcome}'
557	    elif ident['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
558	        # The single most important branch in this file. These bytes are SOMEONE ELSE'S PAPER.
559	        kind, basis = 'wrong_work', ident['basis']
560	    elif ex['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
561	        kind, basis = 'extraction_failure', (
562	            f"stopword density {ex['stopword_density']} < {CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY} — "
563	            f"these bytes are not English prose")
564	    elif chrome_hits >= 2 and (chrome_per_kw >= 5 or (family is None and words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR)):
565	        # WEB FURNITURE WITH NO DOCUMENT UNDER IT. Note that the AEA landing page for Autor (2015)
566	        # carries the article's own "vol. 29, no. 3" citation block -- so journal furniture proves
567	        # only that a page CITES the article, never that it IS the article. This test runs first for
568	        # exactly that reason.
569	        # The LENGTH half of this test is scholarly-only: a 300-word statute section is a statute
570	        # section, and only the chrome DENSITY may call it a web page.
571	        kind, basis = 'landing_page', (
572	            f'{chrome_hits} web-chrome markers ({chrome_per_kw}/1k words) over {words} words — '
573	            f'this is a web page about the document, not the document')
574	    elif family is not None:
575	        # A CASE, A STATUTE, A TRIAL RECORD. Its completeness is not a word count and never was.
576	        kind, basis = family, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — this is its {family.replace("_", " ")}, '
577	                               f'which is COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH (registry stub_floor=None)')
578	    elif ekind == 'journal_version':
579	        kind, basis = 'journal_article', ebasis
580	    elif ekind == 'proceedings_version':
581	        kind, basis = 'proceedings_paper', ebasis
582	    elif ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript'):
583	        kind, basis = ekind, ebasis
584	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR:
585	        kind, basis = 'citation_only', f'{words} words — a citation stub, not even an abstract'
586	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR:
587	        kind, basis = 'abstract', f'{words} words with no version furniture — a stub, not the article'
588	    else:
589	        kind, basis = 'unknown', ebasis
590	
591	    # ---- completeness: THE ONE REDUCER, driven by the registry FOR THIS KIND ---------------------
592	    complete, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, words, ex['verdict'])
593	
594	    return dict(
595	        artifact_kind=kind, artifact_kind_basis=basis,
596	        expression_kind=ekind, expression_kind_basis=ebasis,
597	        complete=complete, incomplete_because=reasons,
598	        n_words=words, sections_present=present, result_bearing_sections=result_secs,
599	        body_ratio=body_ratio, chrome_markers=chrome_hits, chrome_per_1k_words=chrome_per_kw,
600	        extractability=ex, identity=ident, fetch_outcome=fetch_outcome,
601	    )
602

604	# =================================================================================================
605	# 3. THE GRAPH
606	# =================================================================================================
607	
608	class SpanBindingError(ValueError):
609	    """A span that cannot be bound to real bytes. Not a warning: THE BINDING DOES NOT EXIST."""
610	
611	
612	class GraphIntegrityError(ValueError):
613	    """A graph on disk that does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never repaired."""
614	
615	
616	# What it takes to ASSERT that a span survives from one expression into another. Only a claim about
617	# BYTES can do it -- that is the entire content of the word "span-preserving".
618	_AUTHENTICATES = re.compile(
619	    r'byte[- ]for[- ]byte|byte[- ]level|checksum|sha-?256|content[_ -]hash'
620	    r'|identical (?:bytes|text|spans?)|span[- ]level (?:diff|comparison)'
621	    r'|(?:diffed|compared) (?:it )?against the .{0,30}(?:pdf|bytes|text|version)', re.I)
622	_PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES = re.compile(
623	    r'\btitles?\b|\bmetadata\b|\bsimilar\b|\bplausib|\blooks? like\b|self[- ]describ|\bassum', re.I)
624	
625	
626	def authentication_failure(basis: str) -> str:
627	    """'' if `basis` authenticates SPAN PRESERVATION; otherwise the reason it does not.
628	
629	    Sol, verbatim: "A title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT
630	    exact_copy_of."
631	
632	    An ASSERTED span-preserving edge is the ONLY thing in this graph that lets a span name a document
633	    WHOSE BYTES WE DO NOT HOLD. It is therefore the only thing worth attacking, and it was guarded on
634	    ONE of its two types: the old check read `type == 'exact_copy_of' and 'title' in basis`. But
635	    `accepted_manuscript_of` is span-preserving too, and it widens attribution identically — so
636	
637	        add_edge(wp, journal, 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'ASSERTED', 'the titles match')
638	
639	    was ACCEPTED, and it made the working paper's spans journal-attributable. The rule is not "no
640	    title matches for exact_copy_of". The rule is: an ASSERTED span-preserving edge must name
641	    BYTE-LEVEL EVIDENCE, whatever it is called. Both directions are enforced here, in ONE predicate,
642	    used by `add_edge()` at construction AND by `from_json()` at load — because an edge that cannot be
643	    constructed but CAN BE LOADED is not a rule, it is a speed bump.
644	    """
645	    if not basis or not basis.strip():
646	        return 'an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it'
647	    weak = _PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES.search(basis)
648	    if weak:
649	        return (f'the basis rests on {weak.group(0)!r}, which proves NOTHING ABOUT BYTES — PEER REVIEW '
650	                f'CHANGES NUMBERS, and matching titles/metadata cannot show a span survived it')
651	    if not _AUTHENTICATES.search(basis):
652	        return ('the basis names no BYTE-LEVEL evidence (a byte-for-byte / checksum / span-level '
653	                'comparison against the target expression). Nothing else can ASSERT span preservation')
654	    return ''
655	
656	
657	@dataclass(frozen=True)
658	class SourcePolicy:
659	    """WHICH EXPRESSIONS THIS ANSWER IS ALLOWED TO CITE. The task's instruction, made mechanical.
660	
661	    This is an ARGUMENT, not a stored field, because admissibility is a property of THE ANSWER and not
662	    of the bytes. The same accepted manuscript is inadmissible under "journal articles only" and
663	    admissible under "any identified version"; a boolean on the node has to be wrong for one of them.
664	    `Expression.citable_in_journal_only_answer` was exactly that boolean, and it is gone.
665	    """
666	    name: str
667	    permitted_expression_kinds: tuple[str, ...]
668	    require_complete: bool = True
669	
670	
671	#: Task 72. "If only the working paper is available, citing it VIOLATES THE JOURNAL-ONLY INSTRUCTION."
672	JOURNAL_ONLY  = SourcePolicy('journal_articles_only', ('journal_version',))
673	PEER_REVIEWED = SourcePolicy('peer_reviewed', ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version'))
674	#: For a legal or clinical question, the authoritative text IS the source. There is no journal here.
675	OFFICIAL_TEXT = SourcePolicy('official_text_only', ('official_text', 'registry_record'))
676	ANY_VERSION   = SourcePolicy('any_identified_version',
677	                             ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version', 'accepted_manuscript',
678	                              'working_paper', 'preprint', 'official_text', 'registry_record'))

681	@dataclass(frozen=True)
682	class Attribution:
683	    """The resolved answer to: may a span from these bytes be attributed AT ALL, and TO WHAT?"""
684	    manifestation_id: str
685	    content_hash: str
686	    expression_id: str                        # the expression whose bytes these ARE
687	    permitted_expression_ids: tuple[str, ...]  # everything a span here may name (IDs — never prose)
688	    policy: str
689	    admitted: bool
690	    names_expression_id: str | None           # THE id the sentence must name. None => name NOTHING.
691	    text: str | None                          # display cache. NOTHING IS EVER VALIDATED AGAINST IT.
692	    refusal: str | None
